chapter_title.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الْإِيمَانَ لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَهُ سَيِّئَةٌ وَ الْكُفْرَ لَا يَنْفَعُ مَعَهُ حَسَنَةٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 209 – The Emān is such that along with it an evil deed will not harm, and the Kufr (disbelief) is such that along with it a good deed will not benefit

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/395)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/395)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) هَلْ لِأَحَدٍ عَلَى مَا عَمِلَ ثَوَابٌ عَلَى اللَّهِ مُوجَبٌ إِلَّا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Yaqoub Bin Shuayb who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘Is there for anyone an Obligated Reward for what he does except for the Momineen?’ Heasws said: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from Ya’qub ibn Shu’ayb who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘Does anyone, other than the believers, has any reward with Allah for his good deeds?’ The Imam said, ‘No, there is no one as such.’”
